intended_use: syngo.CT Lung CAD software device is a Computer-Aided Detection (CAD) tool designed to assist radiologists in the detection of pulmonary nodules during review of multi-detector computed tomography (MD
intended_use_full: syngo.CT Lung CAD software device is a Computer-Aided Detection (CAD) tool designed to assist radiologists in the detection of pulmonary nodules during review of multi-detector computed tomography (MDCT) examinations of the thorax (chest). The software is an adjunctive tool to alert the radiologist to regions of interest (ROI) that may otherwise be overlooked.
target_condition: Pulmonary nodules (solid and subsolid) in chest CT examinations
ground_truth: Reference standard determined by reader consensus: 9 of 18 readers for PMA study cases, 4 of 7 readers for additional cases, and reader study for UCLA cases; 929 true nodules identified across 412 cases from 3560 lung lobes.
other_metrics: The standalone accuracy analysis demonstrated that the sensitivity of VD30 in solid-only mode is not inferior to VD20 in standard mode; the mean number of false positives per subject is significantly lower with VD30 in solid-only mode; sensitivity and mean number of false positives per subject of VD30 in standard mode are not inferior to VD20 in standard mode.
